full_name: Ministry of Tourism, Arts, Culture and Sport
blurb: Champions B.C.'s tourism industry and grows the arts, culture, heritage and creative sectors — while funding amateur sport, advancing multiculturalism and anti-racism, and helping communities host world-class events.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: B.C.'s tourism industry grows and diversifies
goals.description: The ministry works with Destination BC, Indigenous Tourism BC and Regional Destination Management Organizations to grow tourism as a pillar of B.C.'s economy, fund resort communities and events, and help businesses and communities develop new tourism experiences.

main_services.name: Grow and market a tourism business

supporting.name: Get tourism business advice, market data and planning resources
supporting.desc: Destination BC provides tourism business guides, market research, workshops and the Learning Centre to help tourism operators start, manage and grow their business.

mandate_tags.tip: Continue to support tourism as a strong pillar of the B.C. economy

supporting.name: Get guidance to attract tourism investment in your community
supporting.desc: The Tourism Investment Attraction Guidance brings together best practices and resources to help communities attract tourism investment and support sustainable tourism growth.
supporting.users: Local governments, Indigenous communities, tourism organizations

supporting.name: Get help adding tourism experiences to your farm
supporting.desc: The Farm Tourism Guide helps farmers understand the regulatory landscape and opportunities for diversifying their farm with tourism experiences such as tours, tastings and agritourism events.

relationships.service: Protect farmland through the Agricultural Land Reserve

supporting.name: Get micro-grants to build climate resiliency for your tourism business
supporting.desc: The B.C. Tourism Climate Resiliency Initiative, delivered through Destination BC, funds sustainability and climate adaptation planning and micro-grants to implement those plans.

main_services.name: Fund tourism infrastructure and community development

supporting.name: Apply for Resort Municipality Initiative funding
supporting.desc: The Resort Municipality Initiative funds projects in small, tourism-based municipalities to diversify tourism seasons, increase visitation, and improve the sustainability of the tourism sector. RMI funding is part of the ministry's core budget.

supporting.name: Search for tourism and economic development funding
supporting.desc: A searchable funding and grants database helps tourism operators, local governments and Indigenous communities find provincial and other funding opportunities.

channels.label: Economic development funding and grants search tool

supporting.name: Apply to develop or expand a mountain resort
supporting.desc: The Province reviews new mountain resort proposals, major resort expansions and other projects on Crown land under the All-Seasons Resort Policy, working with local and provincial governments to provide timely decisions.

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Arts, culture and heritage thrive in every B.C. community
goals.description: Through the BC Arts Council, Creative BC, Community Gaming Grants and the Archaeology Branch, the ministry funds artists and cultural organizations, grows the creative industries, and protects heritage sites and objects across the province.

main_services.name: Get funding for arts, culture and creative industries

supporting.name: Apply for an arts grant
supporting.desc: The BC Arts Council offers individual arts grants, project assistance and operating assistance grants to artists, collectives and not-for-profit arts and cultural organizations across B.C.

legislation.full_name: Arts Council Act  -  Purposes and powers of the council

supporting.name: Apply for creative industries funding or a motion picture tax credit
supporting.desc: Creative BC, an independent society supported by the Province, delivers funding programs for book publishing, interactive digital media, music, magazine publishing and motion picture, plus the Production Services Tax Credit and Film Incentive BC.

mandate_tags.tip: Continue to support BC's film, television and animation sectors

supporting.name: Apply for a community gaming grant for your nonprofit program
supporting.desc: Community Gaming Grants fund not-for-profit organizations delivering ongoing community programs and services, including arts, culture, sport, and other community benefit categories.

supporting.name: Apply for a heritage conservation grant
supporting.desc: The Heritage Legacy Fund, delivered through Heritage BC, provides grants to conserve and celebrate heritage places, including conservation projects and community heritage planning.
supporting.users: Heritage organizations, local governments, property owners

supporting.name: Get a permit to alter, study or excavate a heritage site
supporting.desc: Archaeological sites on public and private land are protected under the Heritage Conservation Act. A permit is required to alter, excavate or conduct research at a protected heritage site or object.

legislation.full_name: Heritage Conservation Act  -  Permits and heritage protection

supporting.name: Report an archaeological find or artifact
supporting.desc: Anyone who finds an archaeological site, artifact or human remains is asked to report it, and violations of heritage site protection can be reported as a natural resource violation.

supporting.name: Request archaeological information about a property
supporting.desc: Property owners, developers and researchers can request archaeological site information for a property from the Provincial heritage register before planning land use changes.

channels.label: Request archaeological information about a property

main_services.name: Visit and explore B.C.'s cultural institutions

supporting.name: Plan a visit to the Royal BC Museum
supporting.desc: The Royal BC Museum in Victoria is B.C.'s largest museum, with human history and natural history collections including archaeology exhibits.

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: British Columbians participate safely in amateur sport
goals.description: Working through viaSport, the ministry funds provincial sport organizations, athlete development, coach training and community sport programs, and regulates combat sports through the BC Athletic Commissioner.

main_services.name: Get funding and support for amateur sport

supporting.name: Apply for sport participation or performance funding through viaSport
supporting.desc: viaSport allocates provincial government funding to province-wide sport and multi-sport organizations for participation and performance initiatives, and administers grants directly to individuals and community groups.
supporting.users: Sport organizations, athletes, community sport groups

supporting.name: Get a grant to help your child play sport
supporting.desc: KidSport BC offers grants of $150 to $400 to help families of children in financial need pay for registration in local sport activities.

mandate_tags.tip: Continue to support accessibility of amateur sport for all British Columbians

supporting.name: Apply for funding to host a sport event
supporting.desc: Hosting BC (up to $35,000) and the Major Sport Event Hosting program (up to $500,000 annually, grants of $25,000-$150,000) support Canadian championships, world cups and other sanctioned sport events across B.C.

mandate_tags.tip: Leverage major event opportunities like the FIFA World Cup and Invictus Games to maximize economic development and tourism impact

supporting.name: Get coach training and certification
supporting.desc: viaSport delivers National Coaching Certification Program (NCCP) courses, with over 21,000 coaches trained in B.C. since 2013.

supporting.name: Get travel support for amateur athletes and teams
supporting.desc: The BC Ferries Sport Experience, a partnership between viaSport and BC Ferries, provides coastal ferry travel support for amateur sport organizations and athletes travelling to provincial and national championships.

mandate_tags.tip: Continue to support accessibility of amateur sport for all British Columbians

main_services.name: Compete or host events in combat sports safely

supporting.name: Get a licence to promote or compete in professional combat sports
supporting.desc: The BC Athletic Commissioner licenses promoters, contestants, matchmakers, seconds and officials for professional boxing and mixed martial arts, and amateur kickboxing, Muay Thai, MMA and pankration.

supporting.name: Get a permit to host a combat sport event
supporting.desc: A promoter must hold a valid event permit from the BC Athletic Commissioner to hold a professional boxing, MMA or other regulated combat sport contest or exhibition, and follow the applicable unified rules.

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: B.C. hosts world-class events and celebrates its diversity
goals.description: The ministry supports communities hosting sport, arts and cultural events, and leads B.C.'s multiculturalism and anti-racism programs to build a more inclusive province.

main_services.name: Host sport, arts and culture events

supporting.name: Apply for funding to host a major or marquee event
supporting.desc: Marquee event funding supports formal requests of $250,000 or more for high-profile international events such as the FIFA Women's World Cup or major multi-sport events like the North American Indigenous Games.

mandate_tags.tip: Leverage major event opportunities like the FIFA World Cup and Invictus Games to maximize economic development and tourism impact

supporting.name: Get planning resources to host a sport, arts or culture event
supporting.desc: The Hosting Toolkit provides resources to guide event organizers through preparing a bid package, planning logistics, and tracking the economic impact of sport, arts and culture events.

mandate_tags.tip: Support communities in delivering community events such as fairs and festivals that bring people together

supporting.name: Apply for Destination Events Program funding
supporting.desc: The Destination Events Program funds event organizers to enhance B.C.'s reputation as an events destination, foster economic growth and increase visitation across the province.

mandate_tags.tip: Continue to support tourism as a strong pillar of the B.C. economy

supporting.name: Apply for a multiculturalism and anti-racism grant
supporting.desc: Annual grants of up to $5,000, offered under the Multiculturalism Act, support registered non-profits and other eligible organizations running projects that build intercultural understanding or challenge racism and hate.

legislation.full_name: Multiculturalism Act  -  Application for grants / Minister may provide grants

supporting.name: Get anti-racism data, research and reporting supports
supporting.desc: The Anti-Racism Data Act allows government to collect demographic data to identify barriers people face accessing services, with research findings published on racial diversity, education and health outcomes.

supporting.name: Connect with anti-racism and hate crime supports
supporting.desc: Resilience BC: Anti-Racism Network offers a province-wide network of community connections, resources for responding to hate crimes and incidents, and tools to challenge racism.

foundational_services.name: Provincial tourism, arts, culture and sport sector policy and planning
foundational_services.desc: Policy development, sector research, and destination development planning with Destination BC and Regional Destination Management Organizations that underpin the ministry's funding programs across tourism, arts, culture, heritage and sport.

foundational_services.name: Multiculturalism and anti-racism policy, research and annual reporting
foundational_services.desc: Coordinates the annual multiculturalism reporting required of every ministry and Crown corporation under the Multiculturalism Act, and leads anti-racism data and research priorities across government.

mandate_legend.text: Work with the Minister of Finance to review all existing ministry programs and initiatives to ensure they remain relevant, are efficient, and respond to the needs of tourism service providers and arts organizations
Work with communities to support the development of recreation centres, community centres, arts and cultural centres, sports fields and other critical social infrastructure
Support communities in delivering community events such as fairs and festivals that bring people together
Continue to support BC's film, television and animation sectors, and support the Minister of Jobs and Economic Growth to maximize film and motion picture investment and employment opportunities
Continue to support tourism as a strong pillar of the BC economy, sustainably promoting BC's geography and experiences and continuing to support rural and Indigenous tourism opportunities
Continue to support accessibility of amateur sport for all British Columbians
Leverage the opportunities provided by the Invictus Games, the FIFA World Cup in 2026 and other major event bid proposals to maximize the economic development and tourism impact of major events

Support the work led by the Minister of Agriculture and Food to grow the economy by supporting food and beverage producers, farmers, restaurants and the tourism sector with flexible liquor, land use and cannabis regulations
